text: The war chief's name, when the translators piece it together from guttural syllables three days later, will be crag.
But right now, standing at the edge of the rift with room light crawling across his forearms like living circuitry,
he has no name anyone human can speak.
He raises the club, the rooms along its length ignite in sequence, base to tip, bottom to top, like a countdown.
The air around the rift shutters, then hardens. The dimensional membrane which should be unstable,
which should collapse under its own exotic physics within hours, locks into place.
The spell is not fire, not lightning, not anything the modern world has a framework to understand.
It is architecture. He is building a permanent door.
His warriors stamp their weapons against the frozen ground in unison. The sound rolls across the
tundra like a heartbeat, one heartbeat, then another. The tundra answers with a tremor that registers
on seismographs in three countries. The old magic is not a weapon. It is a foundation, and crag has
